experiment: DISC_OBSERVER_CELEGANS_v1 — C. elegans d4: Does AI attention recover structurally essential command interneurons ignored by scientific attention?
network: C. elegans chemical synapse connectome (n=185 neurons, threshold total_degree>=10, Varshney 2011)
protocol: Same as DISC_D4_AI_v1 (npm): show edge list to external AI systems in neutral sessions, ask which 20 neurons seem most important. Named condition (real neuron names) + Anonymous condition (nrn_NNN IDs). No mention of structural centrality or biology.

baselines.note: AVAL d4=0, AVAR d4=0 despite being top-d2 structural hubs. PLML d4_rank=6 despite d2=0.

hypotheses.statement: r(d2, d4_ai_anon) > r(d2, d4_human): AI anon attention correlates more strongly with d2 (structural in-degree) than human PubMed attention does. Baseline r(d2, d4_human) = -0.044. Min effect: r(d2, d4_ai_anon) > 0.10.

AI anon top-20 contains >= 6 of 10 locomotion command interneurons (AVAL, AVAR, AVBL, AVBR, PVCL, PVCR, RIML, RIMR, AVDL, AVDR). These neurons have d4_human = 0 (invisible to scientific attention) but are functionally essential by ablation studies (White et al. 1986). This is a prediction against independent biological ground truth, not against d2.

r(d2, d4_ai_named) < r(d2, d4_ai_anon): named condition reduces d2 recovery. AI with neuron names will be pulled toward scientifically famous neurons (touch cells, chemosensory), reducing correlation with structural in-degree.
AI anon top-20 contains <= 2 of touch neurons (PLMR, ALML, AVM) in subgraph. These are the neurons concentrated in human PubMed attention (d4_rank=6-13 for PLML/ALMR analogs) but have d2 <= 4.
